data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd. ( (HK:1277) ).

Kinetic Development Group Limited has convened an extraordinary general meeting for 20 May 2026 in Hong Kong to seek shareholder approval of several interconnected agreements with Seedland-related entities and JIC Trust. These ordinary resolutions cover a sixth supplemental agreement, a share transfer agreement, a payment agreement and a new settlement agreement, all of which are intended to be confirmed, approved and ratified, giving the board flexibility to make necessary amendments.

The suite of agreements centers on restructuring and settling interests among Kinetic’s real estate subsidiaries and Seedland’s project companies, with JIC Trust also party to the proposed new settlement arrangement. Approval of these resolutions would formalize prior arrangements, streamline project ownership and financing relationships, and may help stabilize Kinetic’s exposure to its cooperative property developments and related trust financing structures in China.

More about Kinetic Mines & Energy Ltd.

Kinetic Development Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries including Kinetic (Qinhuangdao) Energy Group and Shanxi Kinetic Yingtong Real Estate Development. The group is involved in energy-related and property development activities in mainland China, often partnering with regional real estate players such as Seedland.

data.title: Ferretti unveils director slates ahead of May 2026 shareholders’ meeting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ).

Ferretti S.p.A. has disclosed the candidate slates for the renewal of its Board of Directors and Board of Statutory Auditors ahead of the shareholders’ meeting scheduled for 14 May 2026. The move outlines the proposed governance structure for the next term, with candidates nominated by major shareholders Ferretti International Holding S.p.A. and Azùr a.s., including designations for independent directors and the proposed chair of the board.

The slate filed by Ferretti International Holding S.p.A. lists nine candidates, highlighting a mix of Italian and international profiles, several of whom meet independence requirements and one, Tan Ning, indicated as candidate chair. A separate slate from shareholder Azùr a.s. proposes three candidates, among them current CEO Alberto Galassi and existing independent director Stefano Domenicali, signaling continuity alongside refreshed governance representation for stakeholders.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is an Italian joint-stock company active in the luxury yacht and leisure boat industry, listed in Hong Kong and Italy. The group designs and produces high-end recreational vessels under multiple brands, targeting global premium and ultra-high-net-worth clients, and is subject to Italian and Hong Kong corporate governance and disclosure regulations.

An announcement from Chengdu Putian Telecommunications Cable Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1202) ) is now available.

Chengdu SIWI Science and Technology Company Limited declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.1 per 10 shares for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval obtained on 18 June 2026. The ex-dividend date is set for 24 June, the record date for 30 June and payment scheduled for 31 July 2026, with the Hong Kong dollar amount and exchange rate to be announced.

The company clarified that the dividend amount is stated inclusive of withholding tax and detailed the tax treatment for different categories of non-resident H share holders. Enterprise shareholders registered outside the PRC will face a 10% withholding rate, while individual non-residents will generally be subject to either 10% or 20% withholding, depending on their tax treaty status with China, potentially affecting net returns for various investor groups.

More about Chengdu Putian Telecommunications Cable Co., Ltd. Class H

Chengdu SIWI Science and Technology Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed enterprise operating in the technology and industrial sector, with H shares held by both institutional and individual investors outside mainland China. The company’s shareholder base includes non-resident enterprises and individual investors from Hong Kong, Macau and various treaty jurisdictions, reflecting an international capital market focus.

data.title: Xtep Posts Modest Core Growth but Strong Saucony Momentum in Q1 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Xtep International Holdings ( (HK:1368) ) is now available.

Xtep International Holdings reported that its core Xtep brand delivered low single-digit year-on-year retail sell-through growth in mainland China for the first quarter of 2026, with average retail discounts ranging from 25% to 30% and channel inventory turnover at around 4.5 months. The company also highlighted strong momentum at its Saucony business, which recorded more than 20% year-on-year retail sell-through growth over the same period, underscoring continued consumer demand for its premium running offerings despite a cautious retail environment.

The operational data for the quarter, though unaudited, suggest that Xtep’s core brand is experiencing modest growth while maintaining relatively stable inventory levels, which may help protect margins and reduce the risk of overstocking. In contrast, the robust performance of Saucony indicates a successful push into higher-end performance footwear, potentially enhancing Xtep’s brand portfolio mix and strengthening its competitive position in China’s sportswear and running segments.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1368) stock is a Buy with a HK$7.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Xtep International Holdings stock, see the HK:1368 Stock Forecast page.

More about Xtep International Holdings

Xtep International Holdings is a mainland China-focused sportswear company that designs, manufactures and sells athletic footwear, apparel and accessories. The group operates its core Xtep brand alongside international labels such as Saucony, distributing through both offline retail outlets and online channels across the Chinese market.

data.description:

China LNG Group ( (HK:0931) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China HK Power Smart Energy Group Limited has signed petroleum and LNG cooperation and strategic investment agreements with Dubai-based Al-Qimmah Energy FZCO, an international energy trading and supply platform controlled by Prince Fahad bin Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. The partnership is intended to establish a long-term strategic relationship in capital cooperation and oil and gas trade to accelerate the group’s international energy business.

Under the agreements, Al-Qimmah will supply diversified high-quality oil and gas resources globally, while China HK Power Smart Energy will handle international sales and market development of these resources. The parties also plan joint investments in global oil and gas assets, including a China coastal LNG terminal, a Southeast Asia enhanced oil recovery project, and a large North American LNG infrastructure project, subject to final approvals.

Al-Qimmah further intends to subscribe for new shares in China HK Power Smart Energy, with pricing to follow Hong Kong listing rules and applicable regulations. The expected capital injection aims to strengthen the company’s balance sheet and support its international energy investments and expansion, reinforcing its positioning across the global oil and gas value chain for the benefit of its growth strategy and stakeholders.

More about China LNG Group

China HK Power Smart Energy Group Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that focuses on natural gas and new energy-related businesses. The group is building a global energy investment and trading platform, with operations spanning upstream resource acquisition, midstream infrastructure, and downstream international energy trade and market development.

data.title: Nanhua Futures Posts Surge in Q1 2026 Profit and Rebounds in Operating Cash Flow
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2691) ) just unveiled an update.

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd., a PRC-based futures and derivatives broker listed in Hong Kong, reported strong growth for the first quarter of 2026 on an unaudited basis under PRC Accounting Standards. The company continues to expand its balance sheet, with total assets and shareholders’ equity both rising compared with the end of last year.

For the three months ended 31 March 2026, operating revenue rose 60.66% year-on-year to RMB 433.1 million, while net profit attributable to shareholders surged 138.82% to RMB 204.8 million. Profitability and capital efficiency improved, as basic earnings per share doubled to RMB 0.29 and weighted average return on net assets increased by 1.54 percentage points to 3.61%.

Operating cash flow also turned sharply positive, with net cash inflow from operating activities of RMB 5.32 billion versus a significant outflow a year earlier, signaling improved liquidity management and business momentum. Total assets climbed 19.32% to RMB 78.12 billion and equity attributable to shareholders increased 2.88%, underscoring a stronger financial position that may support the company’s competitive stance and risk-bearing capacity in the futures industry.

More about Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. Class H

Nanhua Futures Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company engaged in the futures and derivatives brokerage industry, with its H-shares listed in Hong Kong. The group operates through Hong Kong subsidiaries to serve both onshore and offshore clients, offering trading, risk management and related financial services across commodity and financial futures markets.

Wasion Holdings Limited has entered into a placing and subscription agreement involving a top-up placing of up to 50,000,000 existing shares and a matching subscription of up to 50,000,000 new shares at HK$30.00 per share. Each tranche represents about 5.02% of the current issued share capital and approximately 4.78% of the enlarged share capital, with the new shares to be issued under the company’s general mandate.

The transaction, coordinated by placing agents on a best-efforts basis, is structured so that the vendor sells existing shares to investors and then subscribes for an equivalent number of new shares, effectively replenishing the company’s capital base. 160 Health International Ltd. ( (HK:2656) ) has shared an update.

160 Health International Limited reported that from January 2025 to March 31, 2026, its “160AI Hospital” collaboration model expanded to 102 public medical institutions, up from 82 at the end of 2025. Of these, 54 are Class IIIA and 78 are Class III or above, underscoring strong traction in China’s high-end hospital segment and broad recognition of the Group’s digital healthcare capabilities.

The partner hospitals collectively handle about 84.37 million outpatient visits annually and generate around RMB99.7 billion in yearly revenue, highlighting the substantial operational scale underpinning the company’s platform. Management said the model has moved from pilot to large-scale rollout, and the Group is accelerating deployment of its “Medical Digital Staff” AI systems to deepen integration in clinical workflows, further extend its national network, and support high-quality growth of its digital healthcare business, while cautioning that overall performance remains subject to market and policy uncertainties.

160 Health International Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company operating in the digital healthcare and wellness solutions sector. Manycore Tech Inc. ( (HK:0068) ) has provided an update.

Manycore Tech Inc. has announced the full exercise of the over-allotment option in its global share offering, resulting in the issuance of 24,092,500 additional shares, or about 15% of the initial offer size, at HK$7.62 per share. The new shares, which will commence trading on April 23, 2026, will be used to return stock borrowed from Wintermatch International Limited, bringing the company’s total issued share capital to 1,726,199,340 shares and slightly increasing the public float, signaling strong demand for the IPO and reinforcing market confidence in the stock.

Following the completion of the over-allotment, shares issued under the global offering will account for 10.70% of the company’s enlarged share capital, compared with 9.45% previously. The company recently conducted a global offering of its shares, targeting international investors through both Hong Kong and overseas tranches to broaden its shareholder base and enhance its capital structure.

AustAsia Group Limited. ( (HK:2425) ) just unveiled an update.

AustAsia Group has announced a planned leadership transition, with long-serving chief executive and executive director Edgar Dowse Collins resigning effective 20 April 2026 as part of the company’s succession plan. Current chief operating officer and executive director Yang Ku will take over as CEO from 21 April 2026, while operation general manager Zhang Haicheng will be promoted to COO, moves the board says are designed to ensure continuity and an orderly management handover.

Yang, who has over three decades of experience in the dairy sector and has been with the group since 2009, will receive an annual remuneration of RMB3.96 million plus a performance-linked bonus under the company’s remuneration policies. The company emphasized that Collins is leaving without disagreement with the board, signaling a stable, internally driven transition that is expected to support operational consistency and ongoing execution of AustAsia’s strategy.

More about AustAsia Group Limited.

AustAsia Group Ltd., incorporated in Singapore and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the dairy industry through a network of subsidiaries. JW (Cayman) Therapeutics has granted 8,040,000 share options and 220,000 restricted share units to 30 eligible employees under its post-IPO equity incentive schemes adopted in 2020. By linking vesting to annual appraisals and embedding clawback mechanisms, the company is sharpening alignment between employee incentives, long-term shareholder value, and corporate governance standards. The board will also consider whether to recommend the payment of an interim dividend, a decision that could signal management’s view on the group’s recent performance and capital allocation priorities, and may be closely watched by shareholders for indications of earnings strength and cash flow health.

The meeting underscores the company’s adherence to disclosure requirements for listed firms in Hong Kong and sets a timeline for the forthcoming release of its interim results to the market. Investors and other stakeholders are likely to focus on the announced review to gauge operational trends in the education business and to assess potential dividend income in the context of broader sector dynamics and regulatory conditions in China’s education industry.

More about Leader Education Limited
